section: Chapter 12: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current

questions.question: What is the magnetic effect of electric current?
questions.correct_answer: The production of a magnetic field around a conductor carrying electric current
questions.explanation: When electric current flows through a wire, it produces a magnetic field around it.

questions.question: Who discovered the magnetic effect of electric current?
questions.explanation: Ørsted observed that a compass needle deflected when placed near a current-carrying wire.

questions.question: What are magnetic field lines?
questions.correct_answer: Imaginary lines used to represent the direction and strength of a magnetic field
questions.explanation: These lines show how magnetic forces act around magnets or current-carrying conductors.

questions.question: What does the right-hand thumb rule explain?
questions.correct_answer: The direction of magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor
questions.explanation: If the thumb points in the direction of current, the curled fingers show the magnetic field direction.

questions.question: What is a solenoid?
questions.correct_answer: A long coil of insulated wire that produces a magnetic field when current flows through it
questions.explanation: A solenoid behaves like a bar magnet with north and south poles.

questions.question: What is an electromagnet?
questions.correct_answer: A temporary magnet produced when current flows through a coil around a soft iron core
questions.explanation: It loses its magnetism when the electric current stops.

questions.question: What happens when a current-carrying conductor is placed in a magnetic field?
questions.explanation: The direction of this force depends on the directions of the current and magnetic field.

questions.question: What does Fleming's left-hand rule determine?
questions.correct_answer: The direction of force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field
questions.explanation: Thumb shows force, forefinger shows magnetic field, and middle finger shows current.

questions.question: What is an electric motor?
questions.correct_answer: A device that converts electrical energy into mechanical energy
questions.explanation: Electric motors work on the principle of force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field.

questions.question: What is electromagnetic induction?
questions.correct_answer: The process of generating electric current by changing the magnetic field around a conductor
questions.explanation: This principle is used in electric generators.

notes.title: Magnetic Effect of Electric Current
notes.content: A current flowing through a conductor produces a magnetic field around it.

notes.title: Right-Hand Thumb Rule
notes.content: If the thumb points in the direction of current, the curled fingers show the direction of magnetic field.

notes.title: Solenoid
notes.content: A long coil of insulated wire that produces a magnetic field similar to a bar magnet when current flows through it.

notes.title: Electric Motor
notes.content: A device that converts electrical energy into mechanical energy using magnetic force.

notes.title: Electromagnetic Induction
notes.content: The generation of electric current in a conductor by changing the magnetic field around it.

facts.fact: Electric current flowing through a wire creates a magnetic field around it.
Magnetic field lines form closed loops and never intersect.
A solenoid behaves like a bar magnet when current flows through it.
Electric motors operate using the force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field.
Electric generators produce electricity using electromagnetic induction.
